channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Here Are All the K-Pop Albums That Have Topped the Billboard 200: Full List
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to this week's music update. K-pop is once again dominating the charts, with BTS' new album ARIRANG holding the top spot on the Billboard 200 chart for the third week in a row. This milestone marks a new record for K-pop, as ARIRANG is the first album from the genre to spend three weeks at No. 1."
[Short music transition]
Host: "This achievement is all the more impressive considering that ARIRANG is just one of 23 K-pop albums or EPs to reach No. 1 on the Billboard 200 chart in less than eight years. The first K-pop album to reach No. 1 was BTS' Love Yourself: Tear back in June 2018."
[Short music transition]
Host: "So, which K-pop groups are leading the charge? Stray Kids is currently in the top spot with eight No. 1 titles on the Billboard 200, closely followed by BTS with seven. ATEEZ has two No. 1s, while groups like SuperM, BLACKPINK, and TWICE have each notched one No. 1 to date. Notably, BLACKPINK was the first female K-pop act to land a No. 1 album on the Billboard 200 with their album Born Pink in 2022."
[Short music transition]
Host: "It's worth noting that K-pop groups often have large lineups. For example, TWICE has nine members, while Stray Kids and ATEEZ have eight. BTS and SuperM each have seven members, and TOMORROW X TOGETHER and NewJeans have five members each. BLACKPINK, on the other hand, has four members. Interestingly, no K-pop solo artists, duos, or trios have topped the Billboard 200 chart yet."
[Short music transition]
Host: "K-pop has been consistently dominating the charts since 2018, with the exception of 2021. In fact, 2023 was the peak year for K-pop albums on the Billboard 200, with five No. 1 albums. We'll be keeping an eye on the charts to see what's next for K-pop. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you in the next update."
[Outro music plays]
